---
title: "Prompting basics"
---

# Prompting basics

::: {.callout-warning}
## Placeholder chapter
Stub. To be drafted via the chapter-writing skill.
:::

## What this chapter will cover

A short, opinionated chapter on talking to a chat AI. Most "prompting guides" you find online are recipes. This one is principles.

- Be specific about the task, the constraint, and the audience.
- Tell the model what *not* to do as well as what to do.
- Give it the data — actually give it. A pasted CSV preview beats three paragraphs of description.
- Iterate; don't redraft. AI is good at fixing one thing at a time.
- When in doubt, ask the model what it would need to do better. The answers are useful.

## Where this chapter fits

Right after the LLM glossary and before the chapters where we start working in chat seriously (Part II).

## TODO for the author

- A worked before/after example: vague prompt, output, sharper prompt, better output.
- One "anti-pattern" sidebar: roleplay prompts ("act as a senior data scientist…") — when they help and when they hurt.
- A short note on the difference between *prompting* and *context engineering*. The book mostly cares about the second one.
